summary: Iteration 7 — Brevo migration verification (Resend → Brevo). 18/18 backend pytest cases PASS. Live Brevo API confirmed for waitlist contact upsert (list=6), waitlist welcome email, OTP magic-link email, admin support notification, and idempotent re-submission. Mocked respx assertions cover payload shape (NAME/FIRSTNAME/LASTNAME/REF_CODE/TIER/AED_BALANCE/SOURCE + listIds=[6] + updateEnabled=true), sender wiring (hello@onexassets.com / OneX Club), replyTo routing, 429 triple-retry, and no-retry on 400. Frontend /dashboard renders cleanly at 1920x900 and 390x844 with 0 console errors for the Surya session. Mobile bottom nav (Home/Properties/Events/Invite/More) confirmed in screenshot.
verified_assertions: import brevo_client + from email_service import _send both succeed at module load.
requirements.txt no longer contains 'resend' (only httpx, already a dep). pip show resend → not installed.

backend/.env: BREVO_API_KEY=xkeysib-..., BREVO_CONTACT_LIST_ID=6, BREVO_SENDER_EMAIL=hello@onexassets.com, BREVO_SENDER_NAME=OneX Club, SUPPORT_INBOX=surya@onexassets.com, SENDER_EMAIL=hello@onexassets.com, PUBLIC_APP_URL=https://www.onexassets.com. RESEND_API_KEY removed.

POST /api/waitlist/join with fresh unique email + ref=surya-378738 → 200 {ok:true, already:false, referrer_name:'Surya'}. Backend logs (live evidence captured): 'brevo contact upserted id=5 email=brevo-it7-wl-… list=6' AND 'brevo email sent … subject=+AED 0 — You\'re on the OneX waitlist done attempt=1' AND admin notification 'brevo email sent … to=surya@onexassets.com subject=[OneX Support] …'.

Repeated POST /waitlist/join with the SAME email → 200 {ok:true, already:true}. No second 'You\'re on the OneX waitlist' welcome was fired for the second call (verified by log scan — only one such line per fresh email). Idempotent at DB level as specified.
POST /api/auth/email/start → 200 {ok:true}; log: 'brevo email sent … subject=Your OneX Club sign-in code attempt=1'.

respx-mocked POST https://api.brevo.com/v3/contacts payload verified: email lowercased; updateEnabled=true; listIds=[6]; attributes={NAME:'Jane Doe', FIRSTNAME:'Jane', LASTNAME:'Doe', REF_CODE:'surya-378738', TIER:'Cadet', AED_BALANCE:250, SOURCE:'framer'}; api-key header xkeysib-…
respx-mocked POST /v3/smtp/email payload: sender.email='hello@onexassets.com', sender.name='OneX Club'; replyTo.email=<member>, replyTo.name=<member name>; subject starts with '[OneX Support]'.
/api/support/contact → 200, support_inbound sent to surya@onexassets.com with subject '[OneX Support] Surya · Iteration-7 brevo smoke from QA' (live log confirms).
Retry: brevo_client.send_email returns messageId after [429,429,201] sequence (3 attempts). On 400, only 1 attempt is made (no retry on non-429 non-2xx). Both verified via respx.
Frontend /dashboard 1920x900 (logged in as Surya): renders 'Welcome back, Surya!', AED 730 balance, 7/12 milestones, Quick Actions. Zero api/network console errors.
Frontend /dashboard 390x844 mobile: renders correctly, bottom nav visible (Home/Properties/Events/Invite/More). Zero console errors.
Email shell now uses https://www.onexassets.com/brand/onex-circle.png as brand mark (replacing inline 1X text).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is now 2064 lines — repeated reminder from iteration 6: split waitlist + referrals + progress + brevo wiring into routers when convenient.

brevo_client.send_email retries on httpx exceptions too (not just 429). Spec says 'On other non-2xx statuses it does NOT retry' which the code respects, but transient network exceptions (e.g. ConnectError) now retry up to 3 times. This is arguably correct/better, but worth documenting so future testers don't flag it.
auth_email_verify imports email_service._send + _shell inside the function body (line 671). Acceptable for hot-path tree-shaking but better as a top-level import for clarity.

Waitlist visitor welcome currently uses send_milestone_done with granted=0 / new_balance=0 — semantically odd (the subject reads '+AED 0 — You\'re on the OneX waitlist done'). Consider a dedicated send_waitlist_welcome template so the inbox subject doesn't say '+AED 0 … done'.

Admin notification body says 'referred by Surya (hello@onex.finance)' — the referrer's email is leaked into the admin inbox. Fine for internal ops, but if SUPPORT_INBOX is ever shared with vendors, sanitize.
seed_data_creation: Live Brevo contacts created in list=6 during test run: brevo-it7-wl-a534febecd@onexassets.com (id=5), brevo-it7-wl-4dab0337ae@onexassets.com (id=6), and one brevo-it7-otp-* OTP recipient. Also ~3 transient waitlist_signups rows attributed to Surya. User may clean up via Brevo dashboard if desired.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Regression suite at /app/backend/tests/test_brevo_migration.py — run with `pytest backend/tests/test_brevo_migration.py -v`. Loads /app/backend/.env at top of test for live BREVO_API_KEY access. Live tests hit Brevo's real /v3/contacts and /v3/smtp/email endpoints — each run will create one new contact in list=6 and ~5 outbound emails. respx-based mock tests cover payload shape + retry logic without burning quota. The iteration_6 file's `test_waitlist_and_progress.py` suite is still valid and orthogonal — both can be run together.
rca of the issue: Initial 5 failures (env_brevo_configured + 4 respx tests) had identical RCA: pytest doesn't auto-load backend/.env, so os.environ.get('BREVO_API_KEY') was empty and brevo_client.send_email/upsert_contact early-returned None with 'BREVO_API_KEY not set' warning before reaching the mocked endpoint. Fix: added `load_dotenv(/app/backend/.env)` at the top of the test file. All 18 tests then PASS. No production code changes required.
